title: vachellia bellula
intro: Vachellia bellula, also known as the 'Sweeper Bush', is a small to medium-sized shrub native to the savannas and grasslands of southern Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Vachellia bellula include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea, infused in oil, or used in a salve to harness its medicinal properties.
